title: ServiceRegistry exposes Arc<dyn AnalyticsSink> accessors, leaking Arc into all consumers
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/services/mod.rs:60-71
## Violation
The accessors return references to the internal Arc smart pointer rather than to the trait object. Quoted lines:

    #[must_use]
    pub fn analytics(&self) -> &Arc<dyn AnalyticsSink> {
        &self.analytics
    }

    #[must_use]
    pub fn flags(&self) -> &Arc<dyn FeatureFlagSource> {
        &self.flags
    }

Returning the Arc itself ties callers to the heap-allocation choice and forces them to know whether the registry uses Arc, Box, or a static reference. If the implementation ever switches to a different smart pointer (or to static dispatch via generics), every call site changes. It also lets callers Arc::clone the sink outside the registry's lifetime contract, defeating any future "shutdown all sinks on registry drop" semantics.
## Mandated refactor
Return `&dyn AnalyticsSink` and `&dyn FeatureFlagSource`. If a caller genuinely needs a long-lived handle, add explicit `analytics_arc()` / `flags_arc()` accessors that signal the ownership transfer. Or expose the registry as a thin facade with `record(&self, event)` and `is_enabled(&self, name) -> bool` methods so the Arc never escapes the module.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
